user_message:
Read-only design review. Do NOT edit any files. Return feedback as text only. <task> Second-pass review of the rewritten plan for GitHub issue #41 in /Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext. Plan file: /Users/teslamint/.claude/plans/robust-humming-papert.md Ground ALL findings in actual source code. Read these files before forming any conclusion: - src/entirecontext/db/schema.py (assessments columns, decisions table, FTS triggers, SCHEMA_VERSION location, checkpoints columns) - src/entirecontext/db/migrations/v012.py and migrations/__init__.py (migration pattern) - src/entirecontext/core/decisions.py (create_decision, link_decision_to_file, link_decision_to_checkpoint, link_decision_to_assessment, _tokenize_diff_for_fts, _fts_rank_decisions_from_diff, _apply_staleness_policy trio — exact signatures) - src/entirecontext/core/security.py or wherever filter_secrets lives (confirm symbol + signature) - src/entirecontext/core/telemetry.py (confirm record_operation_event signature) - src/entirecontext/cli/decisions_cmds.py lines 460-588 (_extract_from_session_impl and _get_llm_response signatures) - src/entirecontext/hooks/decision_hooks.py lines 700-774 (maybe_extract_decisions and worker invocation) - src/entirecontext/mcp/tools/decisions.py (register_tools pattern) - tests/test_decision_hooks.py lines ~1300-1450 (7 test methods calling _extract_from_session_impl) - tests/test_decisions_cli.py lines ~470-580 (4 monkeypatch sites) - tests/conftest.py (ec_repo, ec_db, git_repo fixtures) - src/entirecontext/core/checkpoint.py and/or hooks/session_lifecycle.py lines 350-398 (files_snapshot population) - Check if .entirecontext/config.toml is tracked in git (git ls-files .entirecontext/config.toml) </task> <structured_output_contract> Return exactly two sections, targeting 900-1400 words total. SECTION A — Resolution verification of 10 prior findings. For each finding, one line: RESOLVED / PARTIALLY RESOLVED (whats still missing) / NOT RESOLVED.

Prior findings to verify:
1. Critical — Assessment schema mismatch: plan used assessments.rejected_alternatives which does not exist. Actual columns are verdict/impact_summary/roadmap_alignment/tidy_suggestion/diff_summary/feedback/feedback_reason/model_name/checkpoint_id.
2. Critical — Noisy-session quality validation: mocked LLM + golden string comparison is just pipeline pass-through, does not satisfy validated against noisy real-session examples acceptance criterion.
3. Critical — _extract_from_session_impl and _get_llm_response imported/monkeypatched in test_decision_hooks.py (~7 places) and test_decisions_cli.py (~4 places). Deleting them breaks tests immediately.
4. Important — Staleness filter claim was wrong: actual filtering via _excluded_statuses, _staleness_sql_predicate, _apply_staleness_policy with include_stale/include_superseded/include_contradicted flags in core/decisions.py:54-121. PR #56 retrievers use supersession chain collapse.
5. Important — Confirm path loses provenance: plan said candidates create a decisions row but did not promote candidate.checkpoint_id/candidate.assessment_id into decision_checkpoints/decision_assessments via existing join tables.
6. Important — FTS dedup threshold rank > -2.0 is arbitrary and contradicts repo convention. _fts_rank_decisions_from_diff uses normalized min-max rescaling, not absolute rank thresholds.
7. Important — Single mutable confidence column conflates initial score and dedup-penalty-adjusted score; loses auditability.
8. Important — LLM call path lacks redaction. hooks/session_lifecycle.py:211-228 shows convention: run filter_secrets(text, sec_patterns) before backend call.
9. Suggestion — review_status=superseded transition rules are undefined; simpler to drop it in v1.
10. Suggestion — Missing standard infrastructure: telemetry via record_operation_event, typed error classes, config defaults for candidate_dedup_rank_threshold.

SECTION B — New findings introduced by the rewrite. Check each of the following, grounded in actual code:

B1. Migration v013 completeness — Does plan specify both schema.py dict update AND v013.py migration file? Does it account for checkpoints.files_snapshot being parseable JSON? Does it wire FTS5 triggers the same way existing fts_decisions is set up?

B2. checkpoints.session_id — Does the actual checkpoints table have a session_id FK? If not, the plans JOIN for checkpoint-source collection fails at runtime. B3. checkpoint.files_snapshot format — How is files_snapshot actually populated? Is it ever NULL or non-JSON? Does plan need a parse guard? B4. assessments → checkpoints → sessions join — Is assessments.checkpoint_id actually required/non-null? Does the join chain hold in real schema? B5. Dogfood config change — Is .entirecontext/config.toml tracked in git? If yes, committed config drift; if not, framing should differ. B6. Telemetry signature — Does plans claimed record_operation_event(source, operation_name, phase, status, error_class, message) match actual core/telemetry.py signature?

B7. link_decision_to_checkpoint / link_decision_to_assessment — Do these helpers actually exist in core/decisions.py under those names?

B8. Test migration risk — Some tests assert side effects (e.g., decisions.metadata.decisions_extracted marker on session row). Does the retargeting story account for assertion changes, not just call retargeting?

B9. Hook worker subcommand rename — Does plan cover both the hook dispatch side (launch_worker in decision_hooks.py:760-769) and the CLI surface side when renaming extract-from-session to extract-candidates?

B10. Scope-creep re-check — Anything in the plan that should be deferred? Anything out that would leave issue #41 unresolvable?

B11. Any other issues — weird FTS5 trigger interactions, silent dependency on non-existent helpers, etc.

For each finding, state severity: Critical / Important / Suggestion. Cite specific plan section or file:line. If Section A is clean and Section B has no new findings, say so explicitly.
</structured_output_contract>

<grounding_rules>
- Every finding must cite specific file:line or plan section.
- Do not present inferences as facts; label hypotheses clearly.
- Only raise a finding if you can point to a specific code path or plan text that causes the issue.
- Do not flag pre-existing issues unrelated to this plan.
- If a concern cannot be grounded in actual code, omit it.
</grounding_rules>

<default_follow_through_policy>
Read all listed files before forming conclusions. Do not skip any file. If a file does not exist, state that explicitly. Return text only — no file edits.
</default_follow_through_policy>
assistant_summary: 요청 기준에 맞춰 계획서와 지정된 소스 파일들을 전부 먼저 읽고, 실제 코드 시그니처·스키마·테스트 의존성을 기준으로만 2개 섹션 리뷰를 정리하겠습니다. 결론은 파일 근거를 확보한 뒤에만 내겠습니다.
